title: Car Storage Costs: Complete Pricing Guide [2026]
excerpt: How much does car storage cost in 2026? A comprehensive breakdown of national averages, pricing factors, and tips to save money on vehicle storage.
content:
How Much Does Car Storage Cost?

Car storage costs range from as low as $45 per month for basic outdoor parking to $2,000 or more per month for premium concierge storage. The right price for you depends on what you are storing, where you live, and how much protection you need.

This guide breaks down current 2026 pricing across every storage type, explains what drives costs up or down, and shares practical ways to save without sacrificing the protection your vehicle needs.

National Average Prices by Storage Type

Based on current market data from facilities across the United States, here are the typical monthly rates you can expect in 2026:

Outdoor Storage

  • Open lot (fenced, secured): $45 to $100 per month
  • Covered outdoor (carport/canopy): $75 to $150 per month

Outdoor storage is the most affordable option and works well for boats, RVs, trailers, and vehicles that are not highly valuable.

Indoor Storage

  • Basic indoor (warehouse/garage): $100 to $300 per month
  • Shared garage bay: $80 to $200 per month
  • Individual enclosed unit: $150 to $400 per month

Indoor storage provides protection from weather, UV damage, and most pest intrusion. It is the most popular choice for personal vehicles and collector cars.

Climate-Controlled Storage

  • Standard climate-controlled: $150 to $500 per month
  • Premium climate-controlled: $300 to $800 per month

Climate control adds temperature and humidity regulation to indoor storage. This is essential for high-value vehicles, convertibles, and cars stored for extended periods.

Luxury and Concierge Storage

  • Luxury storage: $500 to $1,500 per month
  • Full concierge (exotic/collector): $1,000 to $3,000+ per month

Luxury and concierge storage includes white-glove services such as regular maintenance, detailing, battery tending, enclosed transport, and vehicle preparation. Learn more about luxury car storage options.

What Affects Car Storage Pricing?

Several factors determine what you will actually pay. Understanding them helps you find the best value.

Location

Location is the single biggest pricing factor. A basic indoor spot in Manhattan can cost more than premium climate-controlled storage in a mid-sized Midwestern city. Here is a rough comparison of indoor storage by region:

  • Major metros (NYC, SF, LA, Miami): $250 to $800+ per month
  • Secondary metros (Denver, Nashville, Portland): $150 to $400 per month
  • Suburban and rural areas: $80 to $200 per month

Facilities just outside of major cities often offer the same quality at significantly lower prices. It may be worth storing 30 to 60 minutes outside a major metro to save hundreds per month.

Storage Type

As outlined above, the type of storage you choose has a major impact on price. Moving from outdoor to indoor adds 50% to 100% to the cost, and climate control adds another 30% to 60% on top of that.

Vehicle Size

Larger vehicles take up more space and cost more to store. Standard car spots are sized for sedans and coupes. SUVs, trucks, and especially lifted vehicles may require oversized spots at a premium. RVs, boats, and trailers have their own pricing tiers.

Contract Length

Most facilities offer discounts for longer commitments:

  • Month-to-month: Full rate, maximum flexibility
  • 3-month contract: 5% to 10% discount
  • 6-month contract: 10% to 15% discount
  • 12-month contract: 15% to 25% discount

If you know you will need storage for an extended period, locking in a longer term can save meaningful money over the year.

Amenities and Services

Facilities that offer additional services charge accordingly. Common add-ons and their typical costs:

  • Battery tending: Often included, sometimes $15 to $30/month extra
  • Periodic engine starts: $25 to $50/month
  • Detailing: $100 to $500 per service depending on vehicle size and detail level
  • Enclosed transport: $1.50 to $3.00 per mile
  • Insurance coverage: Varies; some facilities include basic coverage in the monthly rate

Demand and Seasonality

Car storage follows seasonal demand patterns. In cold-weather states, prices tend to rise in late fall as owners put convertibles and classics away for winter. In warm-weather states, summer storage for secondary vehicles can create price spikes. Booking early, before the rush, can help you secure a better rate.

Car Storage Cost by City

Here are typical monthly indoor storage costs for major US cities in 2026:

  • New York City: $300 to $900
  • Los Angeles: $200 to $600
  • Chicago: $150 to $450
  • Miami: $200 to $550
  • San Francisco: $300 to $800
  • Dallas: $100 to $350
  • Seattle: $150 to $450
  • Phoenix: $100 to $300
  • Denver: $125 to $375
  • Atlanta: $100 to $350

Search AutoVault to see real-time pricing from facilities in your specific area.

Tips to Save on Car Storage

1. Compare Multiple Facilities

Prices can vary by 50% or more between facilities in the same area. Always get quotes from at least three options. AutoVault makes it easy to compare side by side.

2. Commit to a Longer Term

If your storage needs are predictable, signing a six- or twelve-month contract typically saves 10% to 25% compared to month-to-month rates.

3. Look Just Outside Major Cities

A facility 30 minutes outside a major metro area often charges 30% to 50% less for equivalent service.

4. Share a Space

Some facilities allow two smaller vehicles to share a larger space at a reduced per-car rate. If you have a friend or family member who also needs storage, this can cut costs significantly.

5. Reduce Your Insurance Costs

Contact your auto insurer about switching to a comprehensive-only policy while your vehicle is in storage. Dropping collision and liability coverage during storage can save $50 to $200 per month on premiums.

6. Skip Services You Can Do Yourself

If the facility charges extra for battery tending, engine starts, or tire checks, consider visiting monthly to handle these tasks yourself. The savings add up, especially over a long storage period.

7. Ask About Off-Season Discounts

Some facilities offer lower rates during their slow season. In northern states, summer may have lower demand. In sunbelt states, winter might be quieter. Ask if any seasonal promotions are available.

Is Car Storage Worth the Cost?

The answer depends on the alternative. If the choice is between a $200/month storage facility and leaving your classic car parked on the street where it faces weather, theft, and vandalism, the storage cost is a bargain compared to the potential losses.

For a rough rule of thumb: if the vehicle is worth more than 100 times the monthly storage cost, the investment makes sense. A $50,000 car stored for $200/month is well protected for a fraction of its value.

Find the Right Price for Your Needs

Car storage is not one-size-fits-all, and neither is the pricing. Define what level of protection your vehicle actually needs, compare options in your area, and negotiate based on the length of your commitment.
